name: 5p15_Cri_du_Chat_terminal_deletion
description: Cri-du-Chat syndrome (5p- syndrome) — terminal 5p deletions (5-40 Mb) causing the characteristic cat-like cry, microcephaly, ID, and distinctive facies. Critical regions: 5p15.3 (cry, CTNND2, TERT region) and 5p15.2 (ID, severe phenotype, SEMA5A, CDH18). Most breakpoints non-recurrent; subtelomeric SD blocks mediate a subset. Complements the existing 5p15_33_PROK2_TERT file with the broader Cri-du-Chat phenotype context.

architecture.internal_structure: Subtelomeric SD cluster (~20-150 kb blocks); two critical regions: 5p15.3 (cat-cry region, contains TERT, SLC6A3, CTNND2 partial, MRPL36); 5p15.2 (severe phenotype, SEMA5A, CDH18, CDH12). Reciprocal 5p duplications also documented but milder.
